name: Alejandro Olmedo
title: Intermediate Software Engineer | Backend Developer | Typescript · Node.js · Cloud
summary: Backend Software Engineer with 5+ years of experience designing, building, maintaining APIs and microservices running on AWS and GCP. Proficient in TypeScript (Node.js runtime), with a proven track record of delivering serverless, event-driven and container-based architectures. Certified as an AWS Developer and GCP Associate Cloud Engineer with a strong foundation in software development best practices, infrastructure as code, observability and CI/CD. A clear communicator dedicated to bridging the gap between technical requirements and product goals to ship scalable software.

bullets: Envolved from the ground up in the full development cycle of a end user smart things software aimed to the canadian market
Worked on a serverless architecture using Typescript as programming language and AWS Lambda, DynamodDB and API Gateway among other AWS services to build both end user and admin services
Implemented a solution using InfluxDB to store end user telemetry data points, then synthetize them to create API response that are used to display time-based charts in the end user mobile app
Persisted microservices architecture using CDK to adhere to the Devops best practices and CICD requirements
Co-liderated a team of engineers to build an event driven microservice using EventBridge Bus and Scheduler to handle the execution on scheduled tasks set by end users and platform events
Reviewed designs, created, prioritized and assigned tasks using Agile methodologies and Kanban boards based on the business impact and the feasibility
Used AWS Cloudwatch to include custom metrics and alarms to monitor the health of the services
Participated in on-call engineer rotations to support the production environment and troubleshoot issues as they arise
Participated in the development of a business to business software used by major telecommunications companies in Canada to manage their internal contractual relationships.
Developed on a microservices event driven architecture using Typescript as programming language and GCP Pub/Sub messaging service and Firestore as a main database
Implemented a cache layer using Redis to greatly improve the performance of a NestJs API taking into account backups and scaling considerations
Continously worked on improving the cloud infrastructure using Terraform and adjusting the Kubernetes configuration to better suit the load of the application

certifications.name: Google Cloud Certified Associate Cloud Engineer
